entry.title: Re: OPA5 vs wdi5 vs Playwright-SAP: pros and cons?
entry.content: Hi @dawid, I can't believe I missed such a detailed answer! Thanks for the thorough explanation. I didn't realize wdi5 was so heavily stressed upon. We have since started implementing Playwright, but since there was a recent change in architecture, it might be worth a shot making pivot - with, as you surmised, a certification in mind. Thanks again for your guidance.

entry.title: Increasing Sales Performance: Why aren’t we talking about the cognitive load of sales travel?
entry.content:
Why aren’t we talking about the cognitive load of sales travel?

I travel a lot for work. In fact, I’m writing this from an aisle seat, drinking mediocre airline coffee. And while I have my packing system down to a science, the amount of mental effort that goes into booking travel is mind boggling. The plethora of small decisions – flight time, airline, hotel cost vs proximity to the office – add to the big ones, like which trips are worth taking because they drive business forward. It takes a lot of mental effort that could have been spent doing much more impactful work.

We spend so much time trying to crack the code of sales productivity. And we should. Non-selling activities still consume 2/3 of the average sales team’s time, according to McKinsey. What if we’re so focused on time savings that we’re overlooking the importance of protecting another precious resource: our sellers’ cognitive power? There’s an obvious productivity killer staring us right in the face:  constant micro-decisions unrelated to selling. These continual small choices might seem insignificant, but in reality when added up, come at a high cost in relation to the mental toll, taxing sales reps unnecessarily each day. .

Context Switching: The Silent Tax on Productivity

Today’s sales landscape requires continual context-switching. Our sellers jump from researching customers to trainings about new products to back-to-back customer calls. Modern CRMs like SAP Sales Cloud keep sellers focused and productive.; we’ve come a long way from the “swivel chair” concept. But to help our sellers be more productive and effective, decreasing context switching and the associated mental stain is critical.

My Product Marketing team just tried an exercise to illustrate the cognitive load of context switching:

  1. First, count aloud by 2s until you reach 20. Easy.
  2. Then, spell the word “strawberry” aloud. Also easy.
  3. Now, alternate one letter and one number: 2-S-4-T-6-R, and so forth. It’s embarrassingly difficult.

If selling is one activity, and booking travel is another, you can imagine how hard a rep’s brain is working during that switch, even if the rep isn't aware of it. But that productivity loss adds up in a big way. There’s a better way: making the task of booking travel as minimally taxing as possible thanks to a little help from our very smart friend:

Offloading Mental Effort, Ramping Up Sales

SAP thinks about these challenges just like you do, because we have sellers and sales leaders we care about, too. We’re integrating Booking Agent into SAP Sales Cloud, so now sellers can use Joule to book travel to visit their clients, directly from the screen where they’re working their deal.

Joule uses the details of the opportunity to create itinerary options for the salesperson to choose from. They can book right there by clicking a button, or they can tell Joule what they want to change. Joule handles all those micro-decisions, freeing up your seller so they can focus on selling.

When you challenge yourself to think differently about what you can do to make space for your sellers to be brilliant, you come to realize that eliminating unnecessary effort is what modern productivity looks like. And yeah, this saves time too.

(Booking Agent within SAP Sales Cloud Generally Available in Q2 2026)

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports For Visual Studio on AWS
entry.content:
You have to use Sessions and Postback methods to keep the report in focus, see this KBA

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1985571

If the reports need more time after the above changes the write a java script to keep pinging the session to keep it active. Sorry no samples...

entry.title: Copy Control Routine 1
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have created a Quantity Contract and assigned a Release Partner AA and AW.

When creating a Sales Order with reference to the Contract the Bill To and Payer is copied from the Contract whereas it should copy the Bill To and Payer from the Customer Master Partner Function of AA and AW.

I am attaching screenshot of VOPAN and the Copy Control for your reference.

Please can someone please provide me a solution.

This is project critical and we are on RISE .

Thanks and Regards.

entry.title: Re: Using Claude Code and Natural Language to Create SAP Datasphere Artifacts
entry.content:
Nice and very infomative blog @Yonggang_Yu 👍

Have you tried to access HDLF space via Datasphere CLI? 

I tried to read HDLF Space definition or local table definition via CLI and unfortunately I'm failing with the error: Failed to read the JSON definition of an object

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
@RubikWal 

single queue in Event Mesh should always have single consumer Iflow.
As I said, there is no way to retry individual message from EM queue. You can filter messages in Iflow (e.g. by checking some fields in payloads) and process only respective ones but you need to fetch all of them via AMQP.

entry.title: Re: New fixes and features in the Pipeline for Cloud Integration package with the Dec 2025 shipment
entry.content:
@alex_bundschuh: Thank you!, now is working!!

BR

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports For Visual Studio on AWS
entry.content: I do have a javascript timer to ping and keep the IIS Session alive. The issue I have is not with the Session. That is still alive. My issue is with the page_load not sending any bytes of data while CrystalReportViewer is querying via ODBC and rendering html. To the load balancer, it looks like an HTTP connection that hasn't sent any data for an hour.

Thanks,

Archana

entry.title: SII tip for Spain #9: ODN not present outgoing invoices created directly in Financials
entry.content:
Welcome to the SII Tips for Spain series. In these short blogs, we share practical insights into specific features of the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SII solution, focusing on the Spanish localization requirements.

Legal Background

In Spain, the invoice ID is defined by three key elements in the SII web service description document:

  • IDEmisorFactura (Invoice Issuer)
  • NumSerieFacturaEmisor (Invoice Number)
  • FechaExpedicionFacturaEmisor (Issue Date)

According to the article 6 of the invoicing regulation in Spain, invoice numbers must be sequential, chronological, and without gaps. To comply with this requirement, SAP provides the Official Document Numbering (ODN) framework.

Additionally, the article 164.Uno.3º of the VAT law mandates that all business-related deliveries of goods or services must be documented with an invoice, even if the transaction is VAT-exempt.

Billing process in Sales and Distribution

When invoices are generated via the billing process in the Sales and Distribution (SD) module:

  • The ODN number is consumed and stored in the Reference field of the accounting document (XBLNR).
  • A compliant invoice printout is generated, which can be sent to the customer in PDF or paper format.
  • The invoice data is submitted to the Spanish Tax Agency (AEAT) via the SII web service, and the output VAT is declared in Form 303 through the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance solution.

This process ensures that both the issuer and the recipient can report the invoice correctly, enabling the Tax Agency to reconcile the transactions based on matching invoice number, date, and issuer tax ID.

Direct Document posting in Financials (Spain)

Since recently, a new generic cross country correspondence template is available in the correspondence feature in the. App “Create Outgoing Invoices“ (S/4HANA Public cloud), that could potentially be used as Invoice, as described in Printing of Customer Invoices in Financial Accounting (FI). Customers need to adapt this template to their business and country regulations. Unfortunately, the ODN for Spain is not enabled in this App, or in the backend transactions FB70 or FB01. For this reason:

  • With the current ODN framework, the ODN number is not consumed, and the official invoice number is not stored in the Reference field (XBRLN) . This may cause the invoice to be rejected by the Tax Agency. Please note that this is a Spain-specific restriction, and in other countries the ODN functionality may be active in Financials
  • The system may use the accounting document number (or any other number) as the invoice number when submitting to SII based on the customizing. Keep in mind that this might not be compliant with the Invoicing regulations about invoice numbering (sequential, chronological and without gaps).

A new  ODN framework (Official document numbering for cross country evaluated receipt settlement) was released in 2025 providing more flexibility in defining the number ranges (for example, by Company Code + Year + Month + Number or any other combination via BRF+ rules) and applications. The link to the help documentation is Configuring System-Generated Official Document Numbers. 

The new ODN framework is not yet compatible with SII, B2G and VERI*FACTU.  Stay tuned to the official channels (What's new, workzone,...) for further news.

 

 

 

Recommendations

To ensure compliance with Spanish regulations:

  • Use the Sales and Distribution module for generating outgoing invoices with a proper official document number.
  • Leverage the ODN framework to maintain sequential numbering and legal traceability.
  • In general, avoid creating "official" invoices directly in accounting since the invoice number will not be consumed from the ODN component.

Further Reading

In case of additional questions, you can visit the SII help documentation in the following links:

  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in ERP

Other SII tips:

  • Spain - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance -> Related Links -> Community section

 

Disclaimer

This blog post reflects the author's interpretation of Spanish legal requirements and SAP system behavior as of the publication date. It is intended for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. For specific guidance, consult your legal or tax advisor and refer to official SAP documentation.

Abdelrani

entry.title: Re: Currency/valuation type does not exist Message No. KM133
entry.content: Removed 'SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance' managed tag as this is a private cloud question.

entry.title: ME21N Enhancement - Disable/Grey Out Custom Subscreen Field Based on PO Document Type
entry.content:
Need help in an enhancement in ME21N / ME22N / ME23N using BAdI ME_GUI_PO_CUST and need guidance on controlling UI behavior dynamically.

Current Setup ---

A custom field (ZZ_ASSET_NO) has been added at PO Header level (EKKO) using CI include (CI_EKKODB)

A custom subscreen has been developed and integrated into the PO Header (Additional Data tab) via BAdI ME_GUI_PO_CUST

The field is displayed and working correctly across: ME21N / ME22N / ME23N

Requirement ---

The requirement is to control the field behavior based on PO Document Type (BSART):

For specific document types (e.g., NB, ZEUP)
Field should be editable

For all other document types
Field should be disabled (greyed out) or optionally hidden

This behavior should be consistent across all three transactions (ME21N / ME22N / ME23N).


Any suggestions, best practices, or references on how this can be achieved would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: I_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEMTEXTTP is empty in the LongText field
entry.content:
Hello SAP, 

We need to use the CDS below, I_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEMTEXTTP, but I realized the LongText record is empty.

Do you have another structure or way to get similar information as this CDS?

Regards

David

entry.title: Re: Configuring OAuth 2.0 and Creating an ABAP Program That Uses OAuth 2.0 Client API
entry.content:
Great blog. 

Has anyone encountered "Error Accessing Secure Store Administration" error?
I believe this is caused by the method call "EXECUTE_CC_FLOW". 
I have this method call inside CATCH block for SET_TOKEN call. 
The problem is that, when my token expires, SET_TOKEN call will throw an exception, and inside the CATCH block, I'm calling EXECUTE_CC_FLOW. I'm guessing that it does a bunch of things at this moment - going out to get token, encrypt it, locks storage, register it into SecureStorage, and if I have a bunch of jobs running simultaneously, and more than one calls EXECUTE_CC_FLOW, it is throwing "Error Accessing Secure Store Administration" error. 

I'm wondering if there's a smart way to handle this in 1709 HANA. 
I could introduce a wait command but trying to see if there's a best practice for this. Thank you.

entry.title: Browser Access Service
entry.content:
I’m trying to configure BAS to map the External URL, but I can’t access it from an external network.

Not even locally.

entry.title: Reimagining Business Processes with Joule Agents - Answers to Your Top Questions
entry.content:
Recently, I had the opportunity to deliver a session titled “Reimagining Business Processes with Joule Agents,” via the AI live enablement session format Beyond Hype. 

After the session, I realized many of the questions raised, especially those related to architecture, integration, governance, and real-world adoption, weren’t just relevant to the live audience—they reflect what many organizations are currently trying to understand as they explore Joule agents more deeply. 

So instead of letting those questions stay in the room, I’m sharing them here. 

Below, I’ve compiled the answers to your top questions—organized into five themes to help you navigate the journey from understanding what Joule agents are, to building and running them, to scaling them with trust across your enterprise. 

The five themes: -  

  • The What — Understanding what Joule agents are and how they fit into your business  
  • The Where — Discovering where agents live in the SAP architecture and how to find them  
  • The How — Getting started, connecting systems, and accessing enterprise data 
  • Enterprise Readiness— Security, governance, reliability, and enterprise-wide adoption 
  • Commercial 

Let's get started!

 

 

The What: Understanding Joule Agents 

 1. How do Joule agents actually fit into my end-to-end business processes?

Joule agents are designed to integrate seamlessly into your business processes - not just as standalone helpers, but as active participants capable of orchestrating and executing complex workflows. 

How Joule agents fit into business processes: 

There are two key patterns: 

  1. Within a single business process: Many agents are designed to handle end-to-end tasks within a specific domain. For example, an accounting accruals agent can research historical data, assess current bank accounts, and propose which accruals should be posted - autonomously completing the entire workflow. 
  1. Across business processes: More sophisticated multi-agent systems can span multiple domains. Imagine a scenario where an agent processes a sales order, posts a goods issue, and then collaborates with a demand planning agent to inform supply chain and procurement decisions. While many agents today focus on specific processes, we're already building toward this cross-functional orchestration. 

 

Now, how do you interact with agents: 

Agents can be interacted with in three ways: 

  • Conversationally (In-Chat): Users can interact with agents via the Joule UI by asking questions or issuing requests.  Here's an example with a Performance Planning Agent. 

 

 

  • Embedded in applications (In-App): Agents can be integrated directly into business contexts. For instance, a customer service agent embedded in a case management tool can automatically analyze an open case, classify it, and suggest relevant knowledge articles - all without the user needing to leave their workflow.  Here's an example of it with CX (Customer Experience) Agents. 

 

 

  • System-Triggered (In-Background): This is where agents become truly proactive. System events rather than user actions can trigger them. For example, our trade classification agent continuously monitors evolving trade policies. When a new regulation affects how your products should be classified, the agent autonomously researches the update, proposes a reclassification, and seeks your approval, ensuring compliance without manual monitoring. 

 

 

The evolution of agents and integration with deterministic automations: 

Joule agents elevate traditional automation by handling the fluid, dynamic variables of your business. While deterministic automation excels at predictable, rule-based tasks, agents can adapt to changing contexts, make informed decisions, and orchestrate next steps intelligently.  

In future scenarios, you'll see hybrid models where agents manage unpredictable elements — like policy changes, customer sentiment, or market shifts — while standard automations handle the known, repeatable steps. Together, they create end-to-end processes that are not just reactive or even proactive, but truly adaptive. 

 

 

The Where: Discovering Agents & Architecture 

 

2. Is there a place to get a complete overview of available AI features and Joule agents? 

Of course there is! Do you know you can explore AI offerings in the AI Catalog on SAP Discovery Center? It shows all out-of-the-box AI Agents and Features by product area and business processes, spanning from Beta, Early Adopter Care, and generally available. Just click on a feature to see further resources, roadmap info, and pricing details. 

Now you might be wondering, what's the difference between an "AI Agent" and an "AI Feature"? 

AI Agent is a dynamic, agentic system designed for end-to-end task completion. It can autonomously execute multiple steps on behalf of the user- leveraging tools, collaborating with other agents, and utilizing existing business context or documents to accomplish complex goals.  

AI Feature, on the other hand, is typically a single-purpose capability. It takes one input and produces one output, such as a text summarization or a sentiment analysis. 

Learn in this video how to navigate the AI Feature catalog and estimate your costs. 

 

 

 

3. Where do Joule agents sit in the overall SAP architecture?   

Joule agents are the latest addition to SAP's AI capabilities, built on top of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).  

Architecturally, they leverage several foundational BTP services and components: 

  • SAP AI Core: Provides the underlying AI infrastructure that powers agent execution and model orchestration. 
  • Joule: Serves a dual role: it acts as the conversational UI through which users interact with agents, and it also functions as an orchestrator that coordinates agent activities and workflows. 
  • Business Applications: Joule agents connect directly to data residing in SAP's cloud business applications (such as S/4HANA Cloud, SuccessFactors, Ariba, etc.), enabling them to operate within a real business context. 
  • SAP Business Data Cloud: For customers who have adopted it, Joule agents can also tap into this layer for broader access to harmonized and enriched enterprise data. 

In short, Joule agents don't exist in isolation - they sit at the top of SAP's AI stack on BTP, pulling together AI infrastructure, orchestration, conversational interfaces, and enterprise data to deliver intelligent, context-aware automation directly within your business landscape. Check out this blog for more details.

 

 

The How: Getting Started & Connecting 

 
 4. What do I need to get started with Joule agents — from technical prerequisites to activation steps?  

Getting started with Joule agents is straightforward once you understand the discovery and provisioning process. Here's a high-level walkthrough: 

Step 1: Discover Available Agents 

Start by exploring the AI Catalog in SAP Discovery Center. As explained in the first question, there you'll find the complete list of available agents, along with details about their capabilities, availability status (GA, Early Adopter Care, or Beta), and pricing. 

Step 2: Evaluate and Trial 

If you find an agent that fits your needs, check whether it's available for trial. The Discovery Center provides all the necessary information, including technical prerequisites such as BTP subaccount requirements, entitlements, connectivity settings, and configuration details. 

Step 3: Provision the Agent 

Once your organization has purchased the required entitlements, you can provision the agent directly through SAP for Me. We're also introducing enhanced capabilities that will guide you through the provisioning process, including the selection of prerequisite landscapes. BTP will handle the necessary connections in the background, as these are already configured within your BTP landscape- making activation smooth and seamless. 

What if I am still on-premise? Joule agents are cloud-native and run on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). On-premise customers can access these agents by connecting their systems to BTP using integration solutions like SAP Integration Suite or BTP connectivity services. This allows them to leverage the power of AI agents while keeping their core business systems on-premise. 

 
 

 5. How connected are Joule agents across SAP and third-party systems? How are these integrations technically enabled?  

SAP Joule agents are designed to connect to both SAP and third-party systems, including third-party agents. Here is how that connectivity works: 

Agent-to-Agent Communication 

Joule agents use an open standard agent-to-agent (A2A) protocol for communication. This enables both inbound and outbound interactions, meaning: 

  • SAP agents can call external agents 
  • External agents can call SAP agents 

What SAP has added on top of the open standard: 

SAP has introduced two additional primitives to make agent-to-agent communication enterprise-ready: 

  • System and User Context: In an enterprise landscape, an agent may execute a task for a specific organization, using a specific BTP sub-account and tenant. SAP has extended the A2A protocol to include this system context in communication. Additionally, user identity and authorization information are included, so that agents always operate within the user’s access rights on behalf of which they are running. Through an Agent Registry, these agents are made discoverable not only within SAP's ecosystem but also by third-party agents and systems. If you want to learn more about this, you can find information here.  
  • Agent Gateway: Once communication is established, SAP's Agent Gateway ensures that any agent interacting with an SAP agent is securely identified and authenticated. The gateway handles authentication mapping, authorization checks, and routing to the correct agent within the SAP ecosystem. This gateway is part of the Joule orchestrator, which serves as the single entry point for anyone looking to interact with SAP agents. 

Existing integration capabilities remain intact. 

While these primitives enable agent-to-agent communication, nothing changes for existing integration patterns. If you have a third-party or custom agent that needs to interact with a specific SAP system through an API, it continues to work as before. 

For SAP internal agent communication, if you want to reach any SAP internal agent through a third-party agent, the communication will always go through the Joule orchestrator. You can discover information about that SAP internal agent through a central Agent Registry. Once you have identified the specific agent, you can then communicate with it directly by making the call through its ORD endpoint. 

 

 

 6. How do Joule agents access and understand enterprise data?  

Joule agents access enterprise data through multiple mechanisms, depending on the landscape and data architecture in place: 

Data Access Paths 

  • Directly through business applications: Agents can access data from the business application on which they are built, leveraging the data layer it provides. 
  • Through SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC): BDC provides a unified semantic data layer that harmonizes data structures across different SAP applications. This makes data more consistent, better structured, and easier for AI and large language models to understand. BDC also offers a growing library of ready-to-use data products spanning business applications, giving agents consistent, cross-application data access through a single semantic layer. 

How agents understand business context 

  • For standard business applications, the business context is provided directly to the agent as part of its configuration. 
  • With SAP Knowledge Graph, agents also get a richer semantic business context layer, for a more robust, consistent connections between data and its semantic understanding across applications. 

Handling custom extensions 

This is an important topic for many customers. SAP is actively building capabilities that will enable agents to understand customer-specific custom extensions. These extensions will be surfaced to the agent so that it can account for any customizations made within a specific customer landscape. More details on this will be shared in the future as these capabilities mature. 

 

 

Enterprise Readiness 

 

 7. What security, governance, and compliance framework applies to Joule agents?  

Security, governance, and compliance are what make Joule agents enterprise-ready. Here is how SAP ensures this: 

Identity and Access Management 

SAP's own Identity Authentication Service (IAS) provides the foundation for security and governance across Joule agents. It ensures that every user and agent interaction is properly authenticated and authorized within your enterprise landscape. 

Enterprise-Ready Primitives 

As part of the agent-to-agent communication architecture, SAP has introduced two key primitives that reinforce security and governance: 

  • System and User Context: Every agent interaction carries system context (such as the specific BTP sub-account and tenant) along with user identity and authorization information. This ensures agents always operate within the user’s access rights on behalf of whom they are running. 
  • Agent Gateway: The Agent Gateway, part of the Joule orchestrator, ensures that any agent communicating with an SAP agent is securely identified, authenticated, and authorized before it can execute any task. It acts as the central checkpoint for all inbound agent communication. 

Compliance and Observability 

SAP adheres to its established enterprise compliance standards across Joule agents, ensuring that data access, processing, and agent interactions meet the governance requirements that organizations expect from their business software. Additionally, SAP is building observability directly into the agent architecture. This means every agent's actions and decisions are closely monitored and traceable, so for compliance or audit purposes, you can always see exactly what happened under the hood. 

 

 

Commercial 

 8. How are Joule agents licensed and priced — and how can organizations estimate their investment? 

Agent usage is measured in steps, and each step consumes requests from the user’s package or AI Units directly for consumption-based scenarios. 

For users within a Per-User-Per-Month package, Basic Agents consume 5 requests per step, Standard Agents consume 10 requests per step, and Advanced Agents consume 25 requests per step. These requests are drawn from the user’s fair use pool. 

For consumption-based scenarios such as customer-built agents in Joule Studio or agents not included in a package, Basic Agents consume 0.005 AI Units per step, Standard Agents consume 0.01 AI Units per step, and Advanced Agents consume 0.025 AI Units per step. 

Customer-built agents generally fall into the Basic category at present, with fewer steps required for simpler tasks. 

You can find more information here: SAP Business AI Commercial Model 

 

 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
In case you are looking for further resources to learn how to build your own Joule agents, have a look at the curated list of enablement below: 

Here are your key resources to help you get started building your own Joule agents: 

  • How to Get Started with Joule Studio: Prerequisites, Commercials, and Instructions to begin using Joule Studio  
  • Joule Studio Goes Live: Answers to Your Top Questions covering Skills and the new Agent Builder features  
  • Create an Agent in Joule Studio: Step-by-step tutorial on how to build and test an agent in Joule Studio 
  • Best Practices for Building Agents: Key principles and practices for designing effective Joule Agents  
  • Discovery Center Mission: Get Hands-on experience in creating a Joule Agent with multiple skills and MCP 

 

 

Bookmark this blog as we share more information here.

entry.title: Re: Hierarchical Value Help in SAP Fiori Elements – Beyond Flat Value Help
entry.content:
@SunnyTomar0612 
You may have already resolved the issue by now, but if not, we can still take a look.

Thanks for your question. I tried the options mentioned earlier, and they’re working fine on my end. It would be helpful if you could share more details about what you’ve done so far so we can better understand the issue.

Also, please check the console for any errors (for example, ‘node property not defined’) and see if there are failures while loading the value help. Once the annotations and views are set up as described above, it should work as expected.

entry.title: Announcing the First-Ever System-Based SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Certification
entry.content:
On behalf of the Product Learning team at SAP, I am excited to introduce a new way to validate SAP Commerce Cloud skills: a system-based certification that measures your ability to perform real tasks and scenarios in SAP Commerce Cloud. Instead of selecting answers on a theory-only exam, you’ll complete hands-on activities in an SAP-provisioned environment—demonstrating the day-to-day capabilities that matter on live sites. Full details and the latest policies are available on the certification page:

SAP Certified - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud

 

🌟A New Era of Practical, System-Based Assessment

Unlike standard multiple-choice exams, this new certification provides candidates with:
  • A SAP Commerce Cloud system, and
  • A PDF containing a set of realistic tasks to complete
Candidates perform actual configuration and scenario-based exercises directly in the system. Once all required tasks are completed, they can submit their results for automated evaluation. Within minutes, candidates will know whether they have passed or failed — offering a faster, more authentic, and more meaningful way to validate capability.
This practical approach ensures that certified consultants are not only knowledgeable but also skilled in applying SAP Commerce Cloud functionality in realistic customer scenarios.

🎯Why System Based Certifications?

The reimagined SAP Certification exams offer a range of benefits. More specifically:

  • Job-ready proof: Demonstrates you can navigate SAP Commerce Cloud and execute business-critical tasks—not just recall concepts.
  • Strong SAP signal: An SAP-recognized credential and digital badge that hiring managers and project leads across the ecosystem trust.
  • Practice over memorization: Preparation aligns to SAP Learning Journeys and hands-on exercises for better retention and impact.
  • Real-world relevance: Validates cross-functional workflows (catalog, content, search, promotions) that move conversion and revenue KPIs.
  • Always current: Content and recertification align with SAP’s cloud release cycle, reflecting the latest features and UI. 
  • Higher integrity than Q&A-only: Performance on practical tasks reduces guesswork and emphasizes authentic ability.

👤 Who Is This Certification For?

This certification is designed for:

  • Business Users
  • Consultants

It is classified as an intermediate‑level certification and requires approximately 3 hours to complete.

📚How to Prepare

To set yourself up for success, we recommend the following preparation path:

1. Complete the "Configuring SAP Commerce Cloud" Learning Journey

The Learning Journey for Business Users and Consultants on learning.sap.com provide the foundational knowledge required for the hands-on portion of the certification. They combine structured courses with access to practice systems for guided exercises, building familiarity with the interfaces, workflows, and configuration steps assessed.

2. (Optional) Attend the In-Person Classroom Training

For learners who prefer guided instruction, an SAP classroom training session is available. This provides expert-led, structured practice to deepen your understanding and skill level. 

 

📢Spread the Word!

Help us share this exciting update with partners, colleagues, and other stakeholders.

📝We Value Your Feedback

After completing the certification, please take a moment to share your feedback. Your insights help us continuously improve the learning experience.

entry.title: Re: Failed to resolve navigation target "#Bank-manageExte...
entry.content: you have created tile for your extension, you have to add that tile to the role(and assign the role to your user), then you will be able to access it from fiori launchpad

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to Avoide production confirmation consume stock i...
entry.content:
Hello @YangMei1 

Check the note 2988541 - How storage location is determined during production order creation? to understand production storage location determination. Then get your master data right. 

Best regards

Dominik Tyczynski

entry.title: Disallow Budget Save on Exceed Considering Values on the Budget Grouping Instead From Total Budget
entry.content:
Hello,

A client has its budget's groupings configured considering the existent legal entities. Additionally, the configuration does not allow to save the compensation worksheet if the budget exceeds. However, the system only considers the Total Budget when it triggers this budget rule instead of the values present on each budget grouping.

Accordingly with the client, although a planner can have employees assigned from different legal entities, there is a requirement where each legal entity must use its own budget allocated instead of the total budget. In other words, even though the total budget value is ok and it's green or zero, if there is a legal entity which budget group value is exceeding the budget, the system should not allow to save the compensation worksheet until that is corrected.

Is it possible to configure something like this in the SAP SF Compensation Module, or somehow have a workaround in a way that looks to the budget of each legal entity instead the global/total one?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to Avoide production confirmation consume stock i...
entry.content: That might not be sufficient. As production storage location in material master has the lowest priority. Production storage location in bill of material has a higher one and in production supply area the highest.

entry.title: Re: Portugal’s New Deposit & Refund System (SDR): What Businesses and Consumers Need to Know
entry.content:
In purchase order i use a condition type ( delivery Costs )  to represent the value of  deposit . it works fine. But in return process to the vendor i have an error in MIRO transsaction,

Anyone have any sugestions the solve the return process ?

entry.title: Re: Are SAP projects still too customization-heavy?
entry.content: They don't as business has its requirements that need to be implemented. Also functional gaps in SAP standard need to be closed somehow.

Thank you,

Swaminathan. J

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers

entry.title: Manager Rating not available in the summary section after enabling latest experience of PM forms
entry.content:
We have the latest experience of performance management forms enabled in our preview environment. The problem we have is that the manager Rating is not displayed beside the employee's self-rating in the Goals summary section in the employee signature step. Screenshot attached. We are able to see the manager's rating in the side view after clicking on each goal section. Ideally, in the employee signature step, both the self-rating and manager rating should be visible for the employee.

Earlier, when this issue was raised, we were directed to this SAP note saying the fix will be delivered in 2H2025.   https://me.sap.com/notes/3604324/E .

This note has been removed now, but the issue still persists in our preview environment. Not sure if the fix was delivered.  We are waiting for this to be fixed to enable the latest PM forms in production. Is anyone else facing the same problem? Appreciate any guidance on this.

Best Regards, Meera

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning Hub Adoption Sessions 2026
entry.content:
Hi @Dobrov 

I completely understand your frustration in trying to plan your colleagues' exams. Please be assured that all certification exams are scheduled to be released in the new certification exam experience by end of March 2026 - I think only 1 or 2 may be released in April and will provide a greater experience for users.

If your SAP Learning Hub licenses are expiring by the end of March 2026 and you have been unable to take your necessary certification exams due to them not being migrated to the new practical experience, then please create a ticket here: SAP Learning Self Service(s) (login required) to request an extension to your SAP Learning Hub license for the effected users.

entry.title: Re: How does delta works for Datasphere Transformation Flow?

SAP Datasphere – Replication Flow – Loading delta data from SAP S/4HANA – Q & A Session

entry.title: Continuous Performance Management Finish and Add to History Button
entry.content:
Is the Finish and Archive Meeting history a shared action or can the manager and employee archive separately?

We are experiencing, that when an employee completes their meeting documentation and selects the Finish and Add to Meeting History button, the manager goes in later to complete updates and the meeting is in archived and no longer able to go in and make edits. 

 It makes sense to me each should be able to archive separately since Notes are separate.

entry.title: Re: Grouping of Reservations into Purchase Requisition ba...
entry.content:
Hi Caetano, this works from grouping perspective and created PurReq correctly. But got a problem with Pegging (linkage) of PurReq with Original Reservation. Pegging is not correct.

any advise?

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to create separate planned order / purchase requesition for same date
entry.content:
Hi Nikos,

I have requirement to group Reservation into PurReq based on Reservation priority. your solution works works from grouping perspective and created PurReq correctly by clubing / grouping Reservations into PurReq. But got a problem with Pegging (linkage) of PurReq with Original Reservation in MD04. Pegging is not correct.

any advise?

My requirements are:

when I run MRP (md02, md01) in ECC, sap clubs multiple reservations into single purchase requisition because lot size procedure is EX and requirement date is identical for reservations. I have requirement to generate multiple purchase requisitions base on reservations’ priorities as additional parameter. for example,

all 10  reservations have requirement date march 14, 2026. 3 reservations have priority 1, 4 reservations have priority 2, 3 reservations have priority 3. so i want to generate 3 purchase requisitions.

 

Thanks,

 

Sajjad Ahmad

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2023.004 v1.10: novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
Boa tarde! Estamos emitindo NFe para zona franca de Manaus e está retornando a rejeição 652: informado indicador de desoneração inválido para a ZFM, indicando que não estamos enviando no XML, a tag  <indDeduzDeson> igual a 0 ou 1. Detalhe a mensageria não é GRC/DRC.

Utilizamos SAP ECC EHP8, e ao consultar as notas mencionadas neste post (3423950 e 3423949) retorna mensagem que "Não é possível implementar".

Qual a solução para SAP ECC?

entry.title: Re: S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition OData Api Rate Limits
entry.content: Thank you for your feedback. If you agree with my answer, please accept the solution for others in the community.

entry.title: Re: A Simple Guide to ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and the Role of a Cloud Implementation Consultant
entry.content: 😍

entry.title: Load Balancing Options for OData V4 Services – Logon Group Configuration
entry.content:
We are currently working with OData V4 services and have a question regarding load balancing and logon group configuration.

In OData V2, it is possible to assign a logon group at the OData service level, which allows incoming requests to be load-balanced across application servers based on the defined logon group.

However, in OData V4, we do not see a similar option to assign a logon group at the service level. We would like to understand how load balancing is expected to be handled for OData V4 services.Questions:Is there any supported way to assign a logon group to an OData V4 service, similar to what is available for OData V2?If logon group assignment at the OData V4 service level is not supported, is this by design?Are there any alternative or equivalent concepts in OData V4 to control load balancing with logon group?What are the recommended load balancing options or best practices for OData V4 services?If load balancing must be handled outside the OData framework (e.g., via SAP Web Dispatcher, ICM configuration), could you please provide the documentation and recommended approach?We would appreciate if you could provide relevant SAP documentation or guidance on how load balancing is designed and managed for OData V4 services.

entry.title: Re: Category Management with SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Hello  Abhijit,

Thank you for your detailed response and for sharing your experience.

As suggested, we have checked the mentioned points and are currently working on it. We are also verifying the application binding to a service instance, as highlighted in your message. 

Could you please share any relevant document or reference link for these steps? We couldn’t find this information in the configuration guide we are currently following, so any additional guidance would be really helpful.

Thanks again for your support.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Grouping of Reservations into Purchase Requisition based on Reservation Priority as additional p
entry.content: Hi Dominik_Tylczynski, it is business requirement. external Procurement or Stock transfer process is expedited based on priority. Thanks

entry.title: Re: DRC Restriction – Support Document (CS) with Inventory Accounts in Public Edition 2602 (Colombia
entry.content: Dear @adrian321, Support document process (as eDocument type) corresponds to a specific process related with the Purchasing transactions. When you indicate that: "I will try to configure the KR and KG documents to send them as an electronic invoice via DRC and not as a supporting document." can generate additional inconsistencies, because Electronic Invoice process (CO_INV eDoc type) is related with Sales transactions. My recommendation is that you create a customer case under component CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-CO, so we can check in detail the issue and provide a guide to solve it. Thanks in advance and best regards, Liliana Cruz - LPM

entry.title: On Demand Webinar: The Future of Intelligent Hiring with SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Discover how SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors enables organizations to elevate hiring experiences, accelerate recruiting outcomes, and align every hire to business and workforce goals. See how SmartRecruiters works within the SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite to fuel hiring speed, talent quality, and long-term business success.

In this session, we’ll explore the latest trends shaping the future of hiring and showcase AI-first innovations designed to help organizations attract, engage, and hire the skills they need to stay ahead.

Key topics in this webinar include:

  • Emerging trends in talent acquisition and how AI is transforming hiring
  • SAP’s strategic AI and product innovations driving smarter hiring and business decisions
  • How intelligent hiring connects insights, skills, and automation to improve hiring outcomes
  • Real-world examples of how organizations are modernizing hiring across industries, regions, and hiring volumes
  • A live demo of AI-first and agentic capabilities in SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors to attract, hire, and retain top talent

Watch now

entry.title: Re: ATC Check - "Error in the input data: No valid extended types were specified" wht does
entry.content:
Hi Carla,

Have you tried checking the field lengths in your custom program? This error message could be linked to the data elements (field lengths) of the fields. The lengths of some fields have changed while moving to S/4 HANA such as MATNR is 40 char in S/4 HANA. So there may be a similar issue with your program.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: ATC Check - "Error in the input data: No valid extended types were specified" wht does
entry.content:
Hi Carla,

This error could be related to any old ECC data elements or Structures with obsolete field lengths or any hard coded field lengths used in your custom program. Some data elements have changed in S4 HANA, example: MATNR is 40 CHAR . Hence your custom program may not be compatible with S4 HANA version incase of the field lengths and structures.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications
entry.content: Hey @SebastianLoeb sorry for the late reply. The error can be seen on the mobile device when I press there select warehouse button. We have an IDP configured for other BTP applications, LGM uses „pure“ IAS. I didn‘t make any changes to the application in the IAS after it got created while subscribing LGM.

entry.title: Re: I finally found a use for AI in Datasphere
entry.content:
@CLTGravesen - great blog - love the storytelling 😁 

I can certainly relate to the "Works as Designed" paradigme - just had one of those cases myself.

entry.title: CKMLQS wrong quantity structure caused by wrong by-product posting.
entry.content:
hello experts,

We have a problem of wrong actual BOM info.

say, 10 units of Finish goods (FG)  where made of 100 units of semi finish goods (SFG1).

in FG process order, user made a mistake of posting the SFG2 as a by-product. The mistake was reversed. 

Total SFG2 production was 500 units, input materials, say, 700 raw materials.

 

Now, the CKMLQS shows that FG 10 units where made of the same 700 raw  - same details as SFG2. 

My problem is that SFG2 was not consumed against FG (well... it was, but it was reversed) so it should not appear on CKMLQS. 

SAP support argues that it is standard behavior, because there are 2 procurement alternatives, therefore actual bom is counted from the both etc. BUT the output of one of these procurement is ZERO, so even if it is so, system should scale input materialsto zero, because SFG2 consumption was zero.  It scales properly everywhere else. 


So. Question please.  Am I missing something ? 

 

thank you.

Laimonas

entry.content: Please see the following error occuring when trying to log onto Salesforce via Crystal reports 2016. Any help on how to resolve this issue will be really helpful. Thanks in advance to all that reply.

Lot of pointers related to planning and taking into consideration aspects related to FIORI and OData rather than Transaction codes and Autho objects only. Greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Job Approval in SmartRecruiters: Overview, Features, and Configuration Guide
entry.content:
Hi Sachin,

I was going through this and was curious to understand how we can populate approvers based on organizational data, such as Business Unit. I understand that multiple workflows can be created, but this could lead to a large number of workflows if the organizational data is extensive and each Business Unit has different approvers. In SuccessFactors Recruiting, we used business rules to achieve this. How can this be implemented here, especially if requisitions are being created directly in SmartRecruiters?

entry.title: Re: U.S SECURE 2.0 Act - Section 603
entry.content: If an employee is enrolled in regular 401k and roth 401k plans the annual maximum is $24,500.  The system appears to send the catch up amount for regular 401k pre tax to a pre tax catch up bucket.  But shouldn't it send it to the roth catch up bucket?  What happens if that bucket is also maxed?  What happens if they also have regular 457 and 457 roth plans?

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud CLMM alternative
entry.content:
Dear @DKN1,

There is no 1:1 Public Cloud transaction equivalent to CLMM for this requirement. SAP handles material valuation / price changes through dedicated Fiori apps and APIs instead so if the client requirement is to change material valuation/value for existing stock the supported Public Cloud option is Manage Material Valuations (F2680) and the action Change Inventory Prices. SAP explicitly states that this is the Public Cloud functionality similar to MR21

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2670551

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/0152bfb67de346768835bd7ccba3c07c.html

If the material uses price control V and the requirement is to change the Periodic Unit Price, SAP states that this cannot be changed directly in the material master. In that case the supported approach is to use the dedicated material price change app referenced by SAP Note below

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/b5b6fe18f894433c851315695cafd31f.html

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2931804

If the requirement is instead to change price control / price determination logic the supported Public Cloud app is Change Price Determination. SAP documentation includes this app and SAP Note 3421746 confirms its use for changing price control / price determination combinations

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/6cf1d353ca9f4408e10000000a174cb4.html

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3421746

For mass updates / interface-based changes, SAP also provides the Material Price Update API for changing material prices and generating revaluation postings when stock exists

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1e3c2c0366834d1fb76461f439248880/9318a0fe2606406dbaa49246c3d93bde.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/35ecd1fca27b44a5896cbe4311a147a4.html

Summary:

Available options:

  • Manage Material Valuations (F2680) for manual inventory price changes
  • Change Material Costs for V-price / periodic unit price scenarios
  • Change Price Determination for price control / determination changes
  • or the Material Price Update API for mass processing

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: DRC Restriction – Support Document (CS) with Inventor...
entry.content: Hello Liliana, Okay, I will follow your recommendation. Thanks.

entry.title: Cannot assigned different vendor for unplanned delivery cost
entry.content:
Hi,

Can i get some help please.

I have a different vendor for unplanned delivery cost and i want the cost to be part of the material moving ave price during invoicing.

Is this possible?

 


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  1. create PO with no freight price condition
  2. goods receipt
  3. process invoice





------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cannot assigned different vendor for unplanned delive...
entry.content:
Unplanned delivery costs are only entered at the time of invoice posting and are not part of the original purchase order or goods receipt valuation. As per SAP  Help Portal https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/c370b6531de6b64ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US, these costs are either distributed to invoice items or posted to a separate G/L account, and they are not designed to consistently update the material moving average price. Their impact on stock valuation is conditional and depends on factors such as available stock, therefore it cannot be reliably controlled.

To ensure delivery costs are included in material valuation and consistently impact the moving average price, SAP standard requires using planned delivery costs maintained in the purchase order. These costs are considered during goods receipt and properly capitalized into inventory. It is also possible to assign a different vendor (e.g. freight vendor) in this setup.

Therefore, the requested behavior is not supported with unplanned delivery costs. The recommended approach is to use planned delivery costs in the purchase order.

entry.title: Re: How to configure item elective in the learning programm
entry.content:
Hi @Zweli_SARS 

Currently, it's not possible to add curricula to programs.

  • Learning Programs: Bundled Courses in a Syllabus | SAP Help Portal

There have been some enhancements requests on it, and all customers are welcomed to vote for any open requests and/or create additional ones:

  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/278727
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/277327
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/261544

For more details on enhancement requests, please check the KBA 2090228 - How to submit enhancement ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Key figure <> 0 not allowed for unit ' '; see long text
entry.content: Uncheck this option "Check Units for Consistency" in transformation settings. You can see this option under "Edit" tab of transformation.

entry.title: Re: Cierre de Finiquitos!
entry.content: Hola Hugo buenas tardes, para poder calcular un finiquito como comentas en TRM3, ¿cómo debe estar el registro de gestión de nómina?, por ejemplo, en el último periodo calculado al trabajador en el pasado y en exit, o en el periodo actual en exit (¿con retro al último periodo calculado al trabajador?). Muchas gracias

entry.title: Two Days, 160 Minds, One Breakthrough Event: Inside ICPM ’26
entry.content:
ICPM Industry Days 2026: A Breakthrough Moment for the Process Mining Community

This year marked a major milestone for the global process mining community: the very first ICPM Industry Days, hosted in Karlsruhe on March 10–11.
With more than 160 participants, a perfectly balanced mix of business and academic attendees, and even a waiting list due to high demand, the excitement in the air was undeniable.

Day 1: Innovation, Vision, and Big Ideas

The first day set the pace with a bold focus on next‑generation innovation: AI Agents, Object‑Centric Process Mining (OCPM), and the much‑talked‑about Process Atoms.

@SAP Signavio Capability Pitch and Workshop on Process Atoms

The day opened with an energizing session from Fabian Price, who showcased the rapid evolution of SAP Signavio.
From new AI‑embedded features to the introduction of AI Agent Excellence and the future potential of Process Atoms, his presentation clearly demonstrated how Signavio is pushing the boundaries of process intelligence.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Next up, Dr. Peyman Badakhshan delivered a deep, hands‑on exploration of Process Atoms. A session that quickly became one of the hottest topics of the day.
Questions poured in from the audience, sparking a dynamic exchange further enriched by contributions from Prof. Wil van der Aalst and clarifications from Gero Decker.
The message was clear: Process Atoms are opening exciting new possibilities, and customers are already mapping out real‑world use cases.

    

Founders Panel

The grand finale of Day 1 was the highly anticipated Founders Panel, featuring:

  • Gero Decker
  • Marcello La Rosa (Salesforce/Apromore)
  • Martin Klenk (Celonis)
  • Constantin Wehmschulte (MPMX)

The panel dove into the future of process mining, AI‑driven transformation, and how enterprise operations will evolve in the years ahead.
The lively back‑and‑forth, especially between Gero and Marcello,  lit up the room with energy, insight, and laughter. It was a rare chance to see the minds shaping this industry exchange ideas live on stage.

Day 2: Real Customers, Real Applications, Real Impact

The second day shifted gears from visionary thinking to practical transformation stories.
Customers took the stage to share how they are using process mining across different platforms, system landscapes, and industries to improve performance and solve real business challenges.

One standout moment came from Hilti, an SAP Signavio customer, who demonstrated how process mining is helping them increase customer satisfaction and deliver measurable, organization‑wide improvements.
Their story resonated with many attendees and sparked new ideas for applying process intelligence in high‑impact areas.

A Memorable Role Play Session

One of the most engaging moments of the day was the interactive role play delivered by Marcello la Rosa and Marlon Dumas (Apromore).
Their session brought common transformation challenges to life in a dynamic, humorous, and relatable way—highlighting practical vendor–customer interactions, typical pitfalls, and best practices. Attendees described it as both “entertaining and educational,” a combination not easily achieved in technical conferences.

A First Edition That Set the Bar High

From deep technical insights to bold discussions about the future of AI in business, from hands‑on workshops to inspiring customer stories, the ICPM Industry Days 2026, delivered two days of energy, innovation, and community collaboration.

If this first edition is a sign of what’s to come, the future looks incredibly bright for the ICPM community.

And yes… we’re already counting the days until ICPM ’27!

A special shout‑out goes to the powerhouse organizing trio: Julian Lebherz (SAP Signavio), Jean‑Marc Erieau, and Elham Ramezani, whose exceptional work turned this first‑edition event into an instant community favorite.

entry.title: Unlock Intelligent Workflows with Introducing Joule
entry.content:
Joule is SAP’s new way to bring intelligence into daily work across SAP and non‑SAP systems. If you want a clear, fast introduction, start with Introducing Joule. It is a free, beginner‑friendly course that takes about one hour. You will also earn a completion badge when you finish.

In this course, you will see what Joule is and how it delivers connected, context‑aware experiences. You will learn how it supports real business outcomes like higher productivity, scalable automation, and enterprise‑grade trust.

You will meet the core parts of the Joule ecosystem. That includes Joule Skills, Joule Agents, and role‑based assistants. You will also see how different roles work with Joule, and how Joule adapts to each user’s context.

The course explains what you need in place to get started. It covers technical prerequisites and key commercial concepts. It also points you to trusted SAP resources, so you can stay current as Joule evolves.

Want to go further after you finish? Join optional live sessions like “Joule in ABAP,” “Getting Started With Joule Studio,” and “Building an Intelligent Joule Agent using Joule Studio in SAP Build.”

Start learning today. Build your foundation in Joule, and showcase your achievement with a course completion badge.

Introducing Joule

Summary

  • Understand what Joule is and how it powers connected workflows across SAP and non‑SAP systems.
  • Recognize Joule’s business value: higher productivity, scalable automation, and trusted, enterprise‑grade governance.
  • Explain the Joule ecosystem: Joule Skills, Joule Agents, and role‑based assistants.
  • See how roles interact with Joule through context‑aware experiences that adapt to user needs.
  • Identify prerequisites, commercial basics, and trusted SAP resources to adopt and stay current with Joule.

entry.title: Enhancement Request: Time Zone Conversion in Embedded Analytics
entry.content: We request an enhancement in SAP Sales and Service Cloud Embedded Analytics, powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, to ensure proper time zone conversion from UTC to the tenant/user local time zone. Currently, fields such as Created On and Modification Date are displayed one day ahead because timestamps are interpreted in UTC instead of the configured local time (e.g., UTC-06:00 Mexico City), leading to incorrect daily reporting and inconsistent filters like “Today.” According to SAP, DATETIME fields are stored in SAC (Hana Cloud) in UTC, and conversion to the local time zone is not yet supported in Sales and Service Cloud V2 with embedded SAC; although it will be possible to create a calculated column for the local time zone in future releases, this is currently not feasible. This enhancement would ensure accurate date alignment, reliable analytics, and consistency between transactional data and reporting.

entry.title: Mass Change of Batch Status (Restricted/Unrestricted) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, how can we update the batch status between Restricted and Unrestricted use?

We are looking for a way to change this status efficiently, especially in scenarios where multiple batches need to be updated.

Is there a standard functionality available for this, or any recommended approach for performing such updates (including mass changes)?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mass Change of Batch Status (Restricted/Unrestricted)...
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, there is no standard app or functionality that allows you to perform a mass update of batch status (Restricted/Unrestricted).

  • Manual Option

You can change the batch status individually using the Manage Batches app by updating the batch attributes. However, this approach is not suitable for mass processing.

  • Use OData API

For mass updates, the recommended solution is to use the standard OData service API API_BATCH_SRV

This API allows you to update the field 'MatlBatchIsInRstrcdUseStock': 

  • true --> Restricted
  • false --> Unrestricted

Example – PATCH Request

PATCH <host>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BATCH_SRV/Batch(Material='MYMATERIAL',BatchIdentifyingPlant='MYPLANT',Batch='MYBATCH001')
X-CSRF-Token: <token>
Content-Type: application/json
If-Match: <etag>
Accept: application/json

{
  "MatlBatchIsInRstrcdUseStock": false
}

For additional information, please check the following links:

  • Business Accelerator Hub - Batch Master Record
  • SAP Help Portal - API_BATCH_SRV
  • SAP Help Portal - Change a Batch by setting it to restricted use and adjusting the expiration date

Thanks and regards.

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content: Task 3 completed

entry.title: BiWhy – One easy way to reduce Audit DB size by 30–80%: disable User Group Details
entry.content:
Disclaimer:
BiWhy is available again for free to everyone on biwhy.net

User Group Details can be one of the biggest sources of Audit DB bloat

It records USER GROUP NAME and USER GROUP ID for nearly every audited event, including the full nested group hierarchy, while usually adding little practical value. Group membership can be checked directly in CMS, group changes are audited as separate events, and group details are still recorded by the Logon event even when this option is unchecked.

Disabling it can reduce auditing utilization immediately and may reduce Audit DB size over time by 30–80%, depending on system usage, audited events, and group hierarchy depth.

Real system example

Audit DB has only two fact tables:

ADS_EVENT – ~43M rows
ADS_EVENT_DETAIL – ~1B rows
Of these, about 650M rows in ADS_EVENT_DETAIL were just USER GROUP NAME and USER GROUP ID details.

 

Use these queries to check what is driving Audit DB size

Event distribution in ADS_EVENT

SELECT
    Event_Type_ID,
    COUNT(*) AS count,
    CAST(ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS "pct %"
FROM ADS_EVENT
GROUP BY Event_Type_ID
ORDER BY count DESC, Event_Type_ID ASC;

Detail distribution in ADS_EVENT_DETAIL

SELECT
    Event_Detail_Type_ID,
    COUNT(*) AS count,
    CAST(ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS "pct %"
FROM ADS_EVENT_DETAIL
GROUP BY Event_Detail_Type_ID
ORDER BY count DESC, Event_Detail_Type_ID ASC;

 

Screenshots

ADS_EVENT distribution

ADS_EVENT_DETAIL distribution

Example of USER GROUP verbosity

Configuration – User Group Details setting in CMC Auditing

entry.title: Re: Migrating to Seamless Planning in SAP Analytics Cloud: Steps, Pitfalls, and Workarounds
entry.content:
Hello @SJohnson ,

Thank you for this detailed post. Can I please ask 2 questions:

1. About model and public dimension connections and respective import jobs that are used to load transaction and master data from S/4HAHA. For instance, public dimensions: whether we decide to start loading master data from SAP Datasphere or continue loading from S/4HANA in the seamless planning scenario, will these connections and import jobs need to manually be rebuilt?

2. Do you recommend using standard SAP data products as the basis for master and transaction data loads into SAC?

 

Thank you

Kind regards,

Michael

entry.title: SAP Datasphere – Replication Flow – Loading delta data from SAP S/4HANA – Q & A Session
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere – Replication Flow – Loading Delta Data from SAP S/4HANA – Q&A Session

Thank you to everyone who attended yesterday's session — your participation and engagement were greatly appreciated!

 

A quick reminder for our paid subscribers: The live session slides are available for download on learning.sap.com. Simply navigate to My Learning → Recent Sessions to access them. Please note that slides are typically available within a few days of the session.

 

Haven't subscribed yet? Our live sessions are a great way to connect with us virtually, featuring live demos and interactive discussions with fellow participants. We'd love to see you at a future session!

In the meantime, I've compiled some of the key questions and responses from yesterday's discussion on Datasphere – Delta Loading from S/4HANA. Please find the attached txt file for your reference.

entry.title: Cannot restore SAP HANA Database using lob backups
entry.content:
Is it possible restore an SAP PERSON_NAME using only Log backups?


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

With SNAP Centes:

  1. Restore SNAP
  2. Restore Log backups to a local File System
  3. Restore PITR point in time recovery using log backups

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cannot restore SAP HANA Database using lob backups
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thank you for contacting SAP Support.

To give the conclusion first, it's not possible to perform the restore with log backups only.
It's because log backups are dependent on the specific data backups which has been taken previously.
Regarding to your concern, would you please refer to KBA 3556564 - Are log backups required while recovering point in time sap HANA using incremental backups ? 
It's not the exactly same situation as this case.
However, you can have the answer from the statement below in CAUSE section.

"If you wish to recover SAP HANA using differential or incremental backups, you must also use log backups. If log backups are not available, you can only recover using a full data backup."

So, you should prepare the corresponding data backup as well as log backups.
You can check which data and log backups are required for the needed point in time recovery by running the output of hdbbackupdiag OS command.
For details about hdbbackupdiag, please refer to Notes below.

1821207 - Determining required recovery files 
1873247 - Checking recoverability with hdbbackupdiag --check.  

As for how to recover HANA database using a data snapshot, please refer to 'Recover SAP HANA From a Data Snapshot' in HANA Admin guide.

Best regards,
SAP Support
